Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: First I need to understand the DECLARE model structure as described Keys for unary constraints existence absence exactly_one init Values are dictionaries where keys are activities and values support and confidence For binary keys value is dictionary containing as keys the activities and corresponding value support and confidence

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In pm4py DECLARE model typically unary activity support confidence binary source target support confidence so use tuples as keys for binary constraints as standard pm4py format

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Process flow expected 1 IG happens first init 2 IG must exist existence 3 DD happens after IG 4 TFC and CE can happen after DD both needed before PC 5 PC happens after design feasible costs evaluated 6 LT and UT happen after PC 7 AG happens after testing 8 MP happens after approval 9 FL happens last

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Unary constraints init IG existence all activities absence empty exactly_one FL AG

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Binary constraints responded_existence if IG then DD response if A then B after precedence B requires A before succession both coexistence TFC CE LT UT nonsuccession FL IG nonchainsuccession IG FL

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Verify semantics precedence A B means B only if A before succession response precedence good for mandatory sequences coexistence mutual existence

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Refine model avoid redundancy revised distribution succession for adjacent flow response precedence for non adjacent coexistence for pairs nonsuccession for negative

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Double-check pm4py format tuples for binary support 1.0 confidence 1.0 semantics consistent succession covers chain coexistence TFC CE good model solid
